question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all valid contract IDs by probing sequential Long values as contractId and observing cache-hit vs cache-miss timing differences, causing full disclosure of which contract IDs exist in the system without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user retrieve the runtime_bytecode of any contract by supplying an arbitrary contractId Long value, given that the native query 'select runtime_bytecode from contract where id = :contractId' applies no access-control or ownership check, causing full bytecode disclosure of any deployed contract?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a 'deleted' filter in the native query 'select runtime_bytecode from contract where id = :contractId' to retrieve bytecode of a logically deleted contract, causing the EVM to execute code that should no longer be accessible?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user keep stale bytecode alive in the CACHE_NAME_CONTRACT cache indefinitely by repeatedly calling findRuntimeBytecode() with the same contractId before the expireAfterAccess=1h window expires, causing the EVM to execute outdated bytecode even after the contract has been updated in the database?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the CACHE_NAME_CONTRACT Caffeine cache (maximumSize=1000) by issuing findRuntimeBytecode() calls with 1001 distinct contractId values, evicting legitimate cached entries and forcing repeated expensive database queries for high-traffic contracts, causing a denial-of-service via cache thrashing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user pass contractId=null to findRuntimeBytecode(), bypassing the @Cacheable key derivation and triggering a NullPointerException or unparameterized SQL execution against the contract table, causing an application crash or unintended query behavior?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user pass Long.MIN_VALUE or Long.MAX_VALUE as contractId to findRuntimeBytecode(), causing integer overflow or unexpected SQL behavior in the native query 'select runtime_bytecode from contract where id = :contractId', leading to incorrect result or database error?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Cacheable condition 'unless = #result == null' to permanently cache an empty Optional result for a non-existent contractId, causing subsequent legitimate lookups for that ID (after the contract is later deployed) to always return empty from cache for up to 1 hour, resulting in denial-of-service for that contract?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the @Cacheable annotation caches non-null results (including empty Optional), and by pre-populating the cache with empty Optional for a contractId before the contract is deployed, cause the EVM to treat a newly deployed contract as non-existent for up to 1 hour?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable byte[] returned inside Optional<byte[]> from findRuntimeBytecode() to mutate the cached byte array in-place after retrieval, causing all subsequent cache hits for the same contractId to return the attacker-modified bytecode and corrupting EVM execution for other users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a cache key collision in CACHE_NAME_CONTRACT by exploiting the fact that the default Spring @Cacheable key is derived from the single Long contractId parameter, and if two different callers use the same contractId with different contexts, causing one caller to receive another's cached bytecode?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 1-hour expireAfterAccess cache policy on CACHE_NAME_CONTRACT to cause the EVM to execute bytecode from a contract that was deleted or self-destructed up to 1 hour ago, leading to execution of dead contract logic and potential fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user issue a high volume of concurrent findRuntimeBytecode() calls with distinct contractId values to exhaust the maximumSize=1000 cache limit, causing legitimate contract bytecode to be evicted and forcing repeated database round-trips that degrade system performance for all users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on findRuntimeBytecode() to perform a brute-force scan of all Long contract IDs, extracting the runtime bytecode of every deployed contract and enabling reverse engineering of proprietary smart contract logic?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findRuntimeBytecode() returns raw runtime_bytecode bytes with no integrity verification, and if the database row is tampered with (e.g., via a separate vulnerability), cause the EVM to execute attacker-controlled bytecode without detection?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the native query 'select runtime_bytecode from contract where id = :contractId' which selects only the runtime_bytecode column, to infer the existence of a contract row even when the bytecode is null (empty Optional vs. absent row), creating an oracle for contract existence?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Cacheable annotation's use of CACHE_MANAGER_CONTRACT (string 'contract') which shares the same name as CACHE_NAME_CONTRACT (string 'contract'), to cause cache manager resolution ambiguity that results in incorrect cache isolation and cross-contamination of cached bytecode values?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a 'deleted is not true' predicate in the native query to retrieve bytecode of contracts that have been administratively deleted, bypassing the logical deletion mechanism and accessing code that operators intended to be inaccessible?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Caffeine cache uses expireAfterAccess (not expireAfterWrite) for CACHE_NAME_CONTRACT, meaning that a frequently accessed contract's bytecode is never refreshed from the database, causing the EVM to permanently execute stale bytecode after a contract upgrade?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a cache stampede by simultaneously sending many requests for the same uncached contractId, causing multiple concurrent database queries for the same row before the first result is cached, leading to database overload?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Optional<byte[]> return type of findRuntimeBytecode() where an empty Optional (contract not found) is cached the same as a present Optional (contract found with null bytecode), causing ambiguity in downstream EVM logic that distinguishes between 'contract does not exist' and 'contract exists but has no bytecode'?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of bytecode size validation in findRuntimeBytecode() to trigger retrieval of an extremely large runtime_bytecode blob from the database, causing out-of-memory errors or excessive memory allocation in the JVM when the byte[] is loaded into the cache?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findRuntimeBytecode() is called by ContractBytecodeReadableKVState.readFromDataSource() during EVM execution, and by crafting a contract call that references a contractId with a very large cached bytecode, cause excessive memory consumption during EVM execution?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the predictable sequential Long ID scheme for contractId to enumerate all contracts in the system by iterating from 1 to the maximum known entity ID, extracting bytecode for every contract without any authentication or authorization check?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findRuntimeBytecode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a timestamp-based filter in findRuntimeBytecode() (unlike historical query variants in other repositories) to always retrieve the current bytecode regardless of the block timestamp context, causing incorrect historical EVM simulation results?
